title: Markdown preview link enhancement (anchor + SPA nav)

planning_decisions.decision: Use rehype-slug (not rehype-autolink-headings or a hand-rolled walker) for heading id generation
planning_decisions.rationale: rehype-slug is the de-facto plugin for the unified ecosystem and produces GitHub-compatible slugs (the format users already expect from README rendering on GitHub itself). Hand-rolling the walker would re-implement the same edge cases (Unicode normalisation, duplicate-id suffixing) without buying anything. We deliberately do NOT add rehype-autolink-headings — the hover-to-copy anchor link is a separate UX feature and adds visual noise; users get hash-jumping from the TOC link override.
planning_decisions.guided_by: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: '既存資産活用' — extend the existing react-markdown pipeline with a single dependency, don't re-implement

planning_decisions.decision: 4-bucket link classifier (anchor / relative / absolute-same-origin / external) lives in markdown-view.tsx, not a separate util
planning_decisions.rationale: The classifier is short (≈40 lines) and has exactly one consumer. Moving it to a sibling file would add a file to track without buying re-use. If a future viewer (e.g. Notion-style page) needs the same logic we will extract then. Logged here so the next reader knows the option was considered.
planning_decisions.guided_by: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: 'over-abstraction を避ける' — keep code where it lives until it has a second consumer

planning_decisions.decision: Anchor click → pushState (not replaceState as the original spec described)
planning_decisions.rationale: The roadmap text said replaceState, but AC-S027-1-4 explicitly requires browser back/forward to restore previous hashes. With replaceState the back-stack never grows, so back/forward through anchors is impossible. pushState satisfies the AC and matches user expectation from GitHub README rendering. We guard against deepening the stack with a same-hash check (no push if window.location.hash already matches).
planning_decisions.guided_by: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: 'AC > 仕様文' — when the roadmap description and the AC disagree, the AC wins because it is the testable contract

planning_decisions.rationale: S010's `/files/raw` already serves the bytes with the right MIME (S026 added the iframe-friendly raw mode). For an `` element rendered inside the parent palmux2 origin we don't need the same-origin-isolation that /files/preview/{path...} solves for iframes. Using /raw also keeps the cache layer shared with the rest of the Files tab and matches what ImageView already does — one less API surface to teach.
planning_decisions.guided_by: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: '既存資産活用' — reuse the existing endpoint instead of cargo-culting the S026 path-style URL where it isn't needed

planning_decisions.decision: Initial-hash scroll uses double-rAF, not MutationObserver
planning_decisions.rationale: ReactMarkdown renders synchronously inside the component, but heading slug ids only become available after React commits and paints. A double requestAnimationFrame is enough for the second frame to see the mounted DOM, and is far cheaper than wiring a MutationObserver only to tear it down on the first match. We confirmed this works in the E2E (AC-1-5 passes deterministically without flakiness).

implementation_decisions.decision: MarkdownView signature extended with optional repoId/branchId/tabId
implementation_decisions.rationale: Made the new params optional on ViewerProps so other viewers (Image / Drawio / Monaco) and any test-only renderers don't need to know about them. FilePreview passes them in unconditionally now that we plumbed tabId through; legacy callers without the props degrade gracefully (relative links fall back to the browser default — full reload — which is strictly no worse than pre-S027 behaviour).
implementation_decisions.guided_by: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: 'gracefully degrade' — never block the existing call path

implementation_decisions.decision: Add tabId to FilePreview Props (was repoId/branchId only)
implementation_decisions.rationale: Resolving a relative-path link to /{repoId}/{branchId}/{tabId}/{path} requires the tab id at the call site of `useNavigate`. Hard-coding 'files' would break if the tab were ever renamed or if a custom Files-tab provider used a different singleton id. Files-view already knows the tabId (tab registry contract) so plumbing it through one extra prop is cheap and explicit.
implementation_decisions.guided_by: CLAUDE.md: 'タブモジュールシステム — Provider が tabId を所有する'

implementation_decisions.rationale: Cmd/Ctrl/Shift/Alt-click and middle-click should keep their browser-default semantics (open in new tab, open in new window, etc.). We bail out early on non-primary buttons or any modifier so the SPA handler never swallows a user-initiated 'open elsewhere' action. The external-link <a> element keeps its href intact for the same reason.
implementation_decisions.guided_by: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: '明示的 > 暗黙的' — don't override behaviours users are already paying for via keybinds

implementation_decisions.decision: Image src resolved via dirnameOf(currentPath) + normalizePath
implementation_decisions.rationale: We deliberately do not use `new URL(href, base)` because it implicitly anchors to the page's own origin and silently drops segments when the relative path tries to escape the worktree root. Posix-style normalisation keeps `..` segments visible if they would escape, so the API can return a clean 404 rather than the resolver lying about the path.
implementation_decisions.guided_by: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: '責務越境最小' — never let a path-resolution bug cross worktree boundaries

implementation_decisions.rationale: Lets E2E tests target each link bucket (anchor / relative / absolute / external) without relying on href patterns the test would have to keep in sync with fixture content. Costs one attribute per link, zero runtime impact.
implementation_decisions.guided_by: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: 'テスト可能性' — give tests a stable hook

review_decisions.rationale: popstate fires when the user navigates back/forward through pushState entries within the same document; hashchange fires when only the hash differs and the browser short-circuits the history update. React Router intercepts popstate but only re-runs route matching — the URL fragment scroll is our concern, not its. Listening to both ensures AC-1-4 holds across browser quirks (notably Safari, where same-document hash-only popstate can be missed).

review_decisions.decision: classifyLink kept private (de-exported after lint warning)
review_decisions.rationale: react-refresh/only-export-components flagged the dual export. Since the function isn't consumed anywhere else, demoting it to module-private resolves the warning without losing testability — its behaviour is exercised end-to-end via the rendered <a data-link-kind=…> attribute.

test_results.ts_lint: PASS for S027-touched files (markdown-view.tsx, types.ts, file-preview.tsx). 5 pre-existing lint errors in file-preview.tsx / files-view.tsx exist on main and are not introduced by S027.

drift_notes: Dev instance port drifted 8202 → 8203 during sprint due to portman picking the next free port after the kill/restart cycle. Test runner reads PALMUX2_DEV_PORT so no code change required.
Roadmap description suggested history.replaceState for anchor click; AC-1-4 requires back/forward restoration which mandates pushState. Implementation follows the AC. Decision logged above.
backlog_added: Anchor-link copy affordance: GitHub-style hover icon next to each heading that copies a permalink to the clipboard. Out of scope for S027 (UX-only enhancement on top of the slug ids we just added).

Mermaid / KaTeX live render in MarkdownView: now that the rendering pipeline is being touched, several users have asked for inline diagrams. Needs separate sprint with security review (Mermaid eval surface).

Cross-file dirty-buffer warning when navigating via markdown link: today selectFile() warns on dirty drafts but the SPA-link path bypasses it. Low-risk gap — drafts are only created from the Edit toggle, not from preview navigation.
